text: A banana is a yellow fruit that is soft and sweet.
A lemon is yellow and very sour.
A chili pepper is red and extremely spicy.
An apple can be red, green, or yellow and has a sweet taste.
Biryani is a flavorful Indian rice dish made with spices, rice, and usually meat or vegetables.
Samosa is a fried or baked pastry with a spicy potato filling, popular as a snack in India.
Paneer butter masala is a creamy tomato-based curry made with Indian cottage cheese.
Masala dosa is a thin, crispy fermented crepe filled with spicy mashed potatoes.
Chole is a spicy chickpea curry from North India, often eaten with rice or bhature.
Rasgulla is a spongy dessert made from ball-shaped dumplings of chhena cooked in light syrup.
Naan is a soft and fluffy flatbread that is traditionally baked in a tandoor.
Tandoori chicken is chicken marinated in yogurt and spices, roasted in a tandoor.
Gulab jamun is a deep-fried milk-solid ball soaked in sweet syrup, served as dessert.
Pav bhaji is a spicy vegetable mash served with soft buttered buns.
Raita is a yogurt-based condiment that cools down spicy dishes.
Dhokla is a soft and fluffy savory cake made from fermented rice and chickpea flour.
Butter chicken is a rich tomato gravy dish with tender pieces of marinated chicken.
Lassi is a cold yogurt drink blended with sugar, salt, or fruits like mango.
Momo is a type of South Asian dumpling popular in Nepal, filled with meat or vegetables and served with spicy chutney.
Sel roti is a traditional homemade, sweet, ring-shaped rice bread from Nepal.
Hilsa curry is a traditional Bengali dish made with hilsa fish and mustard gravy.
Bhuna khichuri is a spiced rice and lentil dish often served with beef curry in Bangladesh.
Nihari is a slow-cooked stew of beef or lamb, eaten as breakfast in Pakistan.
Chicken Karahi is a spicy curry cooked in a wok-like pan called a karahi, popular in Pakistan.
Pad Thai is a stir-fried noodle dish with shrimp, peanuts, and tamarind sauce, one of Thailand's national dishes.
Tom Yum is a hot and sour Thai soup made with shrimp, lemongrass, and lime juice.
Pho is a Vietnamese noodle soup consisting of broth, rice noodles, herbs, and meat.
Nasi Goreng is Indonesian-style fried rice often cooked with kecap manis, garlic, shallots, and chili.
Rendang is a spicy meat dish slow-cooked in coconut milk and spices, originating from Indonesia.
Nasi lemak is Malaysia's national dish consisting of fragrant rice cooked in coconut milk, served with sambal, anchovies, and egg.
Adobo is a Filipino dish made by marinating meat in vinegar, soy sauce, and garlic, then simmering it.
Halo-halo is a Filipino dessert made from crushed ice, evaporated milk, and mixed ingredients like sweet beans, fruits, and jellies.
Peking duck is a famous Beijing dish featuring crispy roasted duck served with pancakes, hoisin sauce, and scallions.
Kung Pao chicken is a spicy stir-fry dish made with chicken, peanuts, vegetables, and chili peppers.
Mapo tofu is a Sichuan dish featuring soft tofu in a spicy chili and bean-based sauce, typically with minced pork.
Xiao long bao are Chinese soup dumplings filled with meat and hot broth, typically served in a bamboo steamer.
Chow mein is a stir-fried noodle dish made with vegetables and sometimes meat or seafood.
Hot and sour soup is a Chinese soup made with mushrooms, tofu, bamboo shoots, and egg, flavored with vinegar and white pepper.
Dim sum refers to a variety of small Chinese dishes served with tea, including dumplings, buns, and rolls.
Mooncake is a traditional Chinese pastry filled with lotus seed paste or red bean, eaten during the Mid-Autumn Festival.
Kimchi is a traditional Korean side dish made from fermented vegetables, usually cabbage and radish, with chili pepper and garlic.
Bibimbap is a Korean mixed rice dish topped with sautéed vegetables, sliced meat, a fried egg, and chili paste.
Tteokbokki is a popular Korean street food made of chewy rice cakes in a spicy red sauce.
Beef noodle soup is a beloved Taiwanese dish made with braised beef, vegetables, and noodles in a flavorful broth.
Lu rou fan is a classic Taiwanese comfort food of minced pork belly cooked in soy sauce, served over rice.
Bubble tea is a Taiwanese drink made with tea, milk, and chewy tapioca pearls.
Char siu is a Cantonese-style barbecued pork known for its sweet and savory glaze.
Egg tarts are a popular Hong Kong dessert consisting of a flaky pastry crust filled with egg custard.
Wonton noodles are a Hong Kong dish made with thin egg noodles and wontons in a clear broth.
Meat pie is an iconic Australian dish consisting of a flaky pastry filled with minced meat and gravy.
Lamingtons are sponge cakes coated in chocolate and rolled in coconut, a popular Australian dessert.
Pavlova is a meringue-based dessert topped with fresh fruit and cream, considered a national dish of New Zealand.
Hangi is a traditional Māori method of cooking meat and vegetables in an underground oven.
Palusami is a Polynesian dish of taro leaves filled with coconut cream and sometimes meat, baked in banana leaves.
Oka is a Samoan raw fish salad marinated in citrus and coconut milk, similar to ceviche.
Lovo is a Fijian feast where food is wrapped in banana leaves and cooked in an earth oven.
Kokoda is a Fijian dish of raw fish marinated in lime juice and coconut milk, similar to ceviche.
Poke is a Hawaiian dish of diced raw fish, typically seasoned with soy sauce and sesame oil.
Spam musubi is a Hawaiian snack made of rice and Spam wrapped in seaweed.
Loco moco is a Hawaiian dish consisting of rice topped with a hamburger patty, fried egg, and brown gravy.
Sushi is a Japanese dish of vinegared rice combined with seafood, vegetables, and sometimes tropical fruits.
Ramen is a Japanese noodle soup dish with Chinese-style wheat noodles served in a meat or fish-based broth.
Tempura is a Japanese dish of seafood or vegetables that have been battered and deep-fried.
Miso soup is a traditional Japanese soup consisting of a stock called dashi into which softened miso paste is mixed.
Okonomiyaki is a savory Japanese pancake containing a variety of ingredients.
Hummus is a Middle Eastern dip or spread made from cooked, mashed chickpeas blended with tahini, olive oil, lemon juice, and garlic.
Falafel consists of deep-fried balls of ground chickpeas or fava beans, typically served in pita bread.
Shawarma is a Middle Eastern meat preparation where thin slices of lamb, chicken, or beef are stacked and roasted on a vertical rotisserie.
Tabbouleh is a Levantine vegetarian salad made of finely chopped parsley, tomatoes, mint, and bulgur.
Baklava is a rich, sweet dessert pastry made of layers of filo filled with chopped nuts and sweetened with syrup or honey.
Buuz are Mongolian steamed dumplings filled with minced meat, typically mutton or beef.
Khorkhog is a Mongolian barbecue dish where meat is cooked with hot stones inside a sealed container.
Airag is a traditional Mongolian alcoholic drink made from fermented mare's milk.
Boortsog are Mongolian deep-fried cookies often served with tea or used as a dessert.
Suutei tsai is a traditional Mongolian milk tea made with green tea, milk, water, and salt.

Adobo is a iconic Filipino dish considered the unofficial national dish of the Philippines. It is prepared by marinating chicken or pork in a mixture of vinegar, soy sauce, garlic, bay leaves, and black peppercorns, then slowly braised until tender. The vinegar acts as a natural preservative making it last longer without refrigeration. Adobo is deeply rooted in Filipino culture and every family has their own version passed down through generations. It is gluten-free adaptable and pairs well with steamed white rice.

Sinigang is a sour tamarind based soup that is one of the most beloved dishes in Filipino cuisine. It typically contains pork, shrimp, or fish cooked with vegetables such as kangkong, eggplant, radish, and green beans in a tangy tamarind broth. The sourness of the soup is its defining characteristic and can also be made using guava, calamansi, or green mango as souring agents. Sinigang is considered comfort food in the Philippines and is commonly eaten during rainy weather. It is rich in vitamins from the vegetables and is a good source of protein.

Kare-Kare is a rich and hearty Filipino stew made with oxtail, tripe, or beef slow cooked in a thick peanut sauce with vegetables such as eggplant, banana blossom, and string beans. It is traditionally served with bagoong, a fermented shrimp paste that balances the richness of the peanut broth. Kare-Kare is a festive dish often prepared during special occasions and family gatherings in the Philippines. The dish has pre-colonial roots and reflects the indigenous use of peanuts and local vegetables in Filipino cooking.

Lechon is a whole roasted pig that is the centerpiece of Filipino celebrations and fiestas. The pig is stuffed with lemongrass, garlic, onions, and bay leaves then slow roasted over charcoal for several hours until the skin becomes golden and incredibly crispy. Lechon is a symbol of festivity and abundance in Filipino culture and is served at birthdays, weddings, and Christmas celebrations. The Cebu style lechon is particularly famous throughout Asia for its crispy skin and flavorful meat that requires no sauce.

Halo-Halo is a popular Filipino dessert and refreshment made with crushed ice, evaporated milk, and a colorful mixture of ingredients including sweetened beans, coconut strips, plantains, sweet potato, jelly, and topped with ube ice cream and leche flan. The name literally means mix mix in Filipino reflecting how the dessert is enjoyed by mixing all ingredients together before eating. Halo-Halo is especially popular during the hot summer months and represents the diverse and colorful nature of Filipino food culture. It is vegetarian friendly.

Quinoa is a highly nutritious ancient grain originally cultivated by the Inca civilization in the Andes mountains of South America. It is one of the few plant foods that contains all nine essential amino acids making it a complete protein source. Quinoa is also rich in fiber, magnesium, iron, zinc, and antioxidants. It is naturally gluten-free making it an excellent grain alternative for people with celiac disease. One cup of cooked quinoa contains approximately 8 grams of protein and 5 grams of fiber supporting muscle repair and digestive health.

Kale is a dark leafy green vegetable belonging to the cruciferous family and is widely regarded as one of the most nutrient dense foods on the planet. It is exceptionally high in vitamins K, A, and C as well as calcium, potassium, and powerful antioxidants including quercetin and kaempferol. Regular consumption of kale has been associated with reduced risk of heart disease and certain cancers. Kale can be eaten raw in salads, blended into smoothies, or baked into crispy chips. It is vegan, vegetarian, and gluten-free.

Salmon is a nutrient rich fatty fish that is one of the best dietary sources of omega-3 fatty acids, which are essential for brain health, heart health, and reducing inflammation. A 100 gram serving of salmon provides approximately 25 grams of high quality protein along with significant amounts of vitamin B12, vitamin D, and selenium. Regular consumption of salmon has been linked to improved cardiovascular health and cognitive function. Salmon can be grilled, baked, poached, or eaten raw as sashimi. It is gluten-free and suitable for pescatarian diets.

Avocado is a creamy and nutrient dense fruit native to Central America that is rich in heart healthy monounsaturated fats, particularly oleic acid. It contains more potassium than bananas and is an excellent source of vitamins K, C, B5, B6, and folate. The healthy fats in avocado help the body absorb fat soluble nutrients from other foods. Avocado is widely used in salads, sandwiches, smoothies, and as guacamole. It is naturally vegan, vegetarian, and gluten-free making it one of the most versatile healthy foods available.

Lentils are small lens shaped legumes that are among the most nutritious and affordable plant based protein sources available. One cup of cooked lentils provides approximately 18 grams of protein and 16 grams of fiber supporting muscle growth and digestive health. Lentils are also rich in iron, folate, and manganese. They have a low glycemic index making them beneficial for blood sugar management. Lentils are a staple food in South Asian, Middle Eastern, and Mediterranean cuisines. They are naturally vegan, vegetarian, and gluten-free.

Pad Thai is a popular Thai stir fried noodle dish that has become one of the most recognized dishes in the world. It is made with flat rice noodles stir fried with eggs, tofu or shrimp, bean sprouts, and green onions in a flavorful sauce made from tamarind paste, fish sauce, and palm sugar. It is topped with crushed peanuts, lime, and dried chili flakes. Pad Thai was popularized by the Thai government in the 1940s as a national dish to promote Thai identity. It is naturally gluten-free when made with rice noodles.

Paella is a traditional Spanish rice dish originating from the Valencia region that is cooked in a wide shallow pan over an open flame. It is made with short grain rice, saffron, olive oil, and a variety of proteins including chicken, rabbit, seafood, or a combination. The defining feature of paella is the socarrat, the crispy caramelized rice crust that forms at the bottom of the pan. Paella is a communal dish traditionally shared outdoors during family gatherings and celebrations. It is gluten-free and rich in protein and carbohydrates.

Butter Chicken also known as Murgh Makhani is a mildly spiced and creamy Indian curry that originated in Delhi in the 1950s. It is made with tandoor grilled chicken cooked in a rich tomato based sauce with butter, cream, and aromatic spices including garam masala, cumin, coriander, and fenugreek. Butter Chicken is one of the most popular Indian dishes worldwide and introduced many people globally to Indian cuisine. It is best served with naan bread or basmati rice. The dish can be made gluten-free when served without bread.

Tacos are a traditional Mexican street food consisting of a small hand sized corn or wheat tortilla topped with a variety of fillings. Common fillings include grilled meats such as carne asada or al pastor, fresh salsa, guacamole, cilantro, and lime juice. Tacos have deep roots in Mexican indigenous culture and were eaten long before European contact. They are one of the most versatile foods in the world with endless regional variations across Mexico. Corn tortilla tacos are naturally gluten-free and can easily be made vegan with plant based fillings.

Croissant is a buttery and flaky French pastry made using a laminated dough technique where layers of butter are folded repeatedly into the dough to create hundreds of thin crispy layers. Originating in Austria as the kipferl and popularized in France in the 19th century, the croissant has become a global symbol of French bakery culture. It is traditionally eaten at breakfast with coffee or filled with ham and cheese for a savory version. The croissant requires significant skill and time to prepare properly including an overnight resting period for the dough.
